description_long: This factory building features a robust, industrial design with three tall, cylindrical smokestacks rising from a sawtooth-patterned roof. The exterior walls are constructed from concrete or stone, with a dark base and lighter upper sections, giving the structure a utilitarian appearance. The roof is segmented into three distinct sections, each sloping downward and bordered by metal or reinforced edges. Large, rectangular industrial windows are visible on the front facade, providing natural light to the interior. The building sits on a solid, rectangular foundation, and the overall form is symmetrical and functional, typical of early to mid-20th-century industrial architecture. The absence of visible rooftop machinery and loading dock doors suggests a focus on manufacturing or processing rather than logistics.
description: A sturdy, industrial factory building with three tall smokestacks and a sawtooth roof, featuring large front windows and a solid concrete base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cylindrical smokestacks and the stepped, sawtooth roof profile.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the three smokestacks, large industrial windows, and the segmented roof structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the building and the alignment of the smokestacks.
description_view_3: Rear angled view emphasizing the three smokestacks and the geometric roof design.
